full_text:
Dear Jie,

We appreciate the time and effort you invested in our interview process, including the demanding pre-interview task. We have come to a decision after careful consideration, and unfortunately, we have decided to move forward with another candidate for the position.

The decision was difficult to make. Final scores collected from all lab members present in the interviews were close - and high, for both overall and presentation sections. We would like to provide some feedback on your interview, as well as some rationale for our decision.

Feedback on your presentation:

Your presentation was well structured, and followed the instructions that were given.

However, selecting a more focused topic or hypothesis would have clarified your management and communication skills. For example, coming up with insights on a further development of this study or novel methodologies to apply in this setting would have been greatly appreciated by our team.

Answering questions in a more focused manner, and actively asking questions from the team would have showcased your logical thinking and organisational skills.

Rationale on our decision:

Actively asking both general and specific questions regarding, for instance, the start of the employment, the available projects, tasks, etc. would have been a way to signal your motivation towards us.

Other candidate’s background was slightly more aligned with our current needs.
